target.name: Roam Robotics
target.founder: Tim Swift, Mallory Ulaszek, Saul Griffith

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $16M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

data.narrative:
Roam Robotics is a Series A robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $16.4M to date. The valuation assessment draws on a peer universe of nine companies: one public (277810.KQ) and eight private robotics firms at Series B and adjacent stages, including 1X Technologies, Bedrock Robotics, Dexterity, Collaborative Robotics, and Fairmat.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total raised as a proxy for post-money valuation, applies a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A to Series B step-ups, yielding $33M. Peer-median analysis across six same-sector comparables—primarily Series B firms—produces a median valuation of $1.1B, though with substantial dispersion: the peer range spans $300M (Fairmat) to $10.0B (1X Technologies). This wide distribution reflects the heterogeneity of robotics-sector valuations and stage advancement among the reference set.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $28M (low) to $1.2B (high), with a median of $554M. The gap between stage-progression ($33M) and peer-median ($1.1B) reflects the stage discount applied to a Series A company relative to Series B comparables. Key uncertainties include the appropriate stage adjustment factor and the relevance of the Series B peer set to Roam's specific subsector positioning and technology maturity. Additional commercial traction data would refine the median toward either bound.
